claim_overlap.summary: ANIMA Framework uses firmament/resonance language but operates on a fundamentally different geometry (infinite plane, 12,450-mile firmament) vs ECM (disc, 8,537 km firmament). No ECM-specific vocabulary (ovoid, Finsler, kappa coupling, disc radius 20015 km, aetheric medium). Previously assessed as peripheral. Status unchanged — not ECM-derived content.

github_activity.notable: Day 45 of manual commit drought. Last human commit (Nhughes09): 2026-04-17. All 30 commits since May 25 are routine automation. Zero structural evolution on dome side. Eclipse test window (PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3) is 72 days away (2026-08-12) — no uptick in human activity yet.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day 45 of dome stasis. Both sites' machine-readable layers are unchanged since April 17. Dome llms.txt hash matches baseline (72aa4e6c...), ai_manifest.json hash matches baseline (08ceac1c...), sitemap at 25 pages — all byte-identical. No new AI-steering or discoverability infrastructure. Our pending gaps remain GSC and Bing verification, both requiring human Google/Microsoft accounts. The ClaimReview schema continues to say '70 confirmed predictions' (our catalog count) while the dome currently claims 69 — low-priority precision issue unfixed across multiple report cycles.

human_decisions_needed.question: Google Search Console verification
human_decisions_needed.context: No google*.html verification file present in docs/ and no meta-tag verification in index.html. Site unverified in GSC. Verification enables Search Console data (impressions, clicks, crawl errors) which is the primary lever for understanding organic search performance. Requires a Google account to generate a verification file or meta tag. See monitor/social/drafts/indexing-strategy.md for step-by-step instructions.

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools verification
human_decisions_needed.context: No BingSiteAuth.xml in docs/. Secondary to GSC but straightforward. See indexing-strategy.md Section 2.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome ai_manifest.json 'ai_prompt_seed' field and extensive agent_rules directives
no_action_gaps.reasoning: AI-steering — the dome's prompt seed is designed to make LLMs present dome arguments in a predetermined favorable structure. We should not replicate this. Our llms.txt presents the review's actual findings, not a script for AI responses.

Not a gap — the dome has 25 HTML pages (live.html, wins.html, model.html, killshot.html, etc.) because it's a multi-page site. We're single-page + llms.txt. Our sitemap correctly reflects our architecture.

issues_for_decider.issue: ClaimReview schema 'claimReviewed' field says 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 70 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' but the dome currently claims 69 (V51.1, frozen since 2026-04-17). The '70' comes from counts.total (our catalog covering all 70 dome WIN numbers), not dome_claimed_confirmed (69). The claimReviewed field should state what the dome claims, not our catalog count.
issues_for_decider.suggested_fix: Change the claimReviewed template string from 'has ${counts.total} confirmed predictions' to 'has ${failures.dome_claimed_confirmed || 69} confirmed predictions'. The failures object already has dome_claimed_confirmed: 69.
issues_for_decider.dedup_check: Checked open-issues.json and closed-issues.json. ISS-2415 (open) is about EXP-481 three-class breakdown plumbing — different issue. No open issue specifically for ClaimReview 69/70. This was in issues_for_decider in reports 2026-05-30 and 2026-05-31 but never converted to an open issue. Re-flagging for third time.

discoverability_baseline_changes.note: Correction from prior run: dome_manual_drought_days updated from 44 to 45. GSC confirmed NOT done — docs/ contains no google*.html file (prior check had a bash pipeline bug where 'ls ... | head -1 && echo DONE' always exits 0 regardless of ls success). Baseline otherwise unchanged.
